Comment: A-tail (V-tail) fixed-wing on a RealFlight plane model. Geometry lifted from PX4 2106_albatross: split ailerons, two ruddervators that PX4 mixes into pitch+yaw, a wheel and two flaps, all driven by a single motor. The "px4" values are HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS.controls[] indices and MUST match PWM_MAIN_FUNC* in ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d-posix/airframes/1214_flightaxis_atail (and HIL_ACT_FUNC* in init.d/airframes/1214_flightaxis_atail.hil). DESIGN RULE: this table is a PURE IDENTITY PIPE for all twelve channels - rf N == px4 N, RealFlight channel N+1 is driven by PX4 output channel N+1, with no renumbering and no reversal anywhere. WHAT each channel carries is decided ENTIRELY by PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> / HIL_ACT_FUNC<N>, exactly as ArduPilot puts it in SERVOn_FUNCTION. If the wrong thing comes out on a RealFlight channel, change the FUNC parameter - do not re-scramble this table.
ScaleComment: The "scale" column is the ONE thing here that is not free configuration, because it is a signal-domain conversion rather than a preference. PWMSim emits non-reversible Motor functions as [0,1] and EVERYTHING else as [-1,1] (PWMSim.cpp:77-88), while RealFlight wants [0,1] on every channel. So "unipolar" (pass through) is correct if and only if you assigned a Motor to that PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N>, and "bipolar" ((v+1)/2) is correct for servos, control surfaces and peripherals. Get it wrong and the failure is quiet: a motor read as bipolar idles at half throttle and never stops, and a surface read as unipolar loses its entire negative half and only ever deflects one way. RULE: change "scale" on a row whenever you change that channel's FUNC between a Motor and anything else. The values below match the FUNC assignments the shipped airframe sets.
ReverseComment: There is deliberately no "reverse": true anywhere in this file. Direction is not a property of the pipe, it belongs to whoever configures the aircraft, and there are two places to set it - both outside this file. (1) PX4 side: the PWM_MAIN_REV parameter is a bitmask over output channels, bit N-1 for channel N, and it IS honoured in SITL - it is applied in MixingOutput::output_limit_calc_single (mixer_module.cpp:531) which runs upstream of PWMSim, so it reaches RealFlight. Reverse channel 2 with 'param set PWM_MAIN_REV 2'; channel 9 is bit 8, i.e. 256. (Note PWM_MAIN_MIN/MAX are a different story - PWMSim overwrites them with setAllMinValues/setAllMaxValues, so per-channel endpoint trimming does NOT work in SITL.) (2) RealFlight side: each servo has its own direction in the model's servo setup. SYMPTOM of a wrong direction: the surface moves the correct amount but the wrong way, so the aircraft diverges instead of correcting - a reversed elevator pitches down when PX4 commands nose-up.
TailComment: rf1/rf3 (RF ch2/ch4) carry the two ruddervators of the A-tail: PWM_MAIN_FUNC2 = 203 Servo 3 (CS2, left) and PWM_MAIN_FUNC4 = 204 Servo 4 (CS3, right). PX4 does the V-tail mixing in the control allocator (CS2/CS3 declare both a pitch and a yaw torque, types 13/14), so the two surfaces already carry the correct pitch+yaw blend and RealFlight must NOT mix them again - configure each tail surface in the RealFlight model as an independent servo, not a linked V-tail pair. If pitch or yaw comes out inverted, reverse that ONE channel (PWM_MAIN_REV bit 1 for ch2, bit 3 for ch4) rather than editing this table.
AuxComment: rf8-rf11 (RF ch9-ch12) are mapped but carry no function in the shipped airframe: PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 and up are unset, so PX4 emits 0.0 on those outputs and the bipolar conversion turns that into a steady 0.5 - neutral, and byte-identical to the UnmappedDefault these channels used to fall back on. They are listed anyway so that using one takes a parameter change and nothing else: set PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> to Flaps, Landing_Gear, a Servo, a Peripheral via Actuator Set, or anything else PX4 offers, and it appears on RealFlight channel N with no edit to this file. Remember the scale rule above if what you assign is a Motor.
RfModel: informational only - aircraft is selected inside RealFlight

Channels.comment: v-tail left    <- controls[1]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC2 = 203 Servo 3 (CS2) - see TailComment
v-tail right   <- controls[3]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC4 = 204 Servo 4 (CS3) - see TailComment
unassigned     <- controls[8]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 to use
unassigned     <- controls[9]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC10 to use
unassigned     <- controls[10] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC11 to use
unassigned     <- controls[11] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC12 to use
